questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the Winter Fog Experiment (WiFEX):
1. It is a long-term open-field experiment focused solely on fog.
2. It is spearheaded by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) under the Ministry of Science and Technology.
3. One of its key objectives is to develop now-casting of winter fog for the next 6 hours.

How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: WiFEX is one of the world's few long-term open-field experiments focused solely on fog. Statement 2 is incorrect: It is led by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM) under the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES), not the IMD under the Ministry of Science and Technology. Statement 3 is correct: Its primary objective is to develop better now-casting (next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.
questions.takeaway: WiFEX is an MoES initiative led by IITM, designed to improve the now-casting and forecasting of winter fog through open-field experiments.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: One of the world's few long-term open-field experiments focused solely on fog. Institutions: Led by Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology under the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES). Objective: To develop better now-casting (next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: In the context of the Winter Fog Experiment (WiFEX), the meteorological term "now-casting" primarily refers to:

options.text: Predicting weather phenomena for the upcoming season based on historical climate data.
Forecasting weather conditions for the immediate future, typically up to the next 6 hours.
Real-time monitoring and mitigation of particulate matter pollution in urban areas.
Long-term climate modeling to assess the impact of global warming on winter fog.

questions.explanation: In meteorology, now-casting refers to forecasting weather conditions for the immediate future. In the context of WiFEX, its specific objective is to develop better now-casting (for the next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.
questions.takeaway: Now-casting is a short-term weather forecasting technique. WiFEX utilizes this to predict winter fog for the immediate 6-hour window.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Objective: To develop better now-casting (next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the core mandate of the Winter Fog Experiment (WiFEX) led by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology?

options.text: To conduct closed-laboratory simulations for analyzing the chemical composition of urban smog.
To deploy cloud-seeding technologies for the artificial dispersal of dense fog over major transportation hubs.
To carry out a long-term open-field study aimed at developing better now-casting and forecasting of fog.
To formulate policy guidelines under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change for mitigating winter pollution.

questions.explanation: WiFEX is one of the world's few long-term open-field experiments focused solely on fog. It is led by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (under the Ministry of Earth Sciences) with the objective of developing better now-casting (for the next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog. It is an observational and predictive initiative, not a mitigation, artificial dispersal, or closed-laboratory project.
questions.takeaway: WiFEX is an open-field observational experiment under MoES aimed at improving the short-term prediction (now-casting) of fog, distinguishing it from pollution mitigation or cloud-seeding programs.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: One of the world's few long-term open-field experiments focused solely on fog. Institutions: Led by Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology under the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES). Objective: To develop better now-casting (next 6 hours) and forecasting of winter fog.
